page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a base clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics. The single-core performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls within the average tier, aided by the 3.1 GHz clock speed and support for AVX-512 vector instructions. However, multi-core execution and memory-intensive operations, such as large-block memory reads and writes, perform in the lower percentiles. This performance profile suggests a bottleneck when handling highly parallelized tasks or large datasets in memory, as evidenced by lower scores in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.hfc6.xlarge offers a dedicated compute resource profile without the overhead of bundled local storage or high memory capacities. This makes it a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads that require consistent CPU performance but have low memory footprints. Appropriate workloads include lightweight web serving, single-threaded application servers, basic text processing, and small-scale utility tasks that benefit from dedicated Intel Xeon cores without demanding high memory throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 3.1 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance, supported by AVX-512 instruction sets, but weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This hardware configuration represents a cost-effective entry point for compute-focused workloads with low memory requirements. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and basic utility applications that require dedicated CPU allocation without the premium cost of high-density memory configurations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a balanced, low-memory footprint for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPUs clocked at 3.1 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
No local storage and 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core performance with AVX-512 instruction support
Suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks
